first_paragraph: ***Leucadendron burchellii***, the **Riviersonderend conebush**, is a flower-bearing shrub that belongs to the genus *[Leucadendron](/wiki/Leucadendron "Leucadendron")* and forms part of the [fynbos](/wiki/Fynbos "Fynbos"). The plant is native to the [Western Cape](/wiki/Western_Cape "Western Cape"), [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa").

***Leucadendron burchellii***, the **Riviersonderend conebush**, is a flower-bearing shrub that belongs to the genus *[Leucadendron](/wiki/Leucadendron "Leucadendron")* and forms part of the [fynbos](/wiki/Fynbos "Fynbos"). The plant is native to the [Western Cape](/wiki/Western_Cape "Western Cape"), [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leucadendron_burchellii&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

The shrub grows 1.6 m (5.2 ft) tall and flowers in August. The plant dies after a fire but the seeds survive.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The seeds are stored in a toll on the female plant and fall to the ground after two months. The seed is then spread by ants that carry it to their nests. The plant is [unisexual](/wiki/Plant_reproductive_morphology "Plant reproductive morphology") and there are separate plants with male and female flowers, which are pollinated by small beetles.

In Afrikaans, it is known as the *Riviersonderend-tolbos*.

## Distribution and habitat

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leucadendron_burchellii&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Distribution and habitat")]

The plant occurs in the [Riviersonderend Mountains](/wiki/Riviersonderend_Mountains "Riviersonderend Mountains") from [Jonaskop](/w/index.php?title=Jonaskop&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jonaskop (page does not exist)") to [McGregor](/wiki/McGregor,_South_Africa "McGregor, South Africa"). The plant grows on northern slopes in rocky or sandy sandstone soils at altitudes of 800 to 1,000 m (2,600 to 3,300 ft).
